title: yueyang130/DeeR-VLA
abstract:
Official code of paper "DeeR-VLA: Dynamic Inference of Multimodal Large Language Models for Efficient Robot Execution"

DeeR-VLA: Dynamic Inference of Multimodal Large Language Models for Efficient Robot Execution ( NeurIPS 24 ) Yang Yue 1   Yulin Wang 1   Bingyi Kang 2   Yizeng Han 1   Shenzhi Wang 1   Shiji Song 1   Jiashi Feng 2   Gao Huang 1 1 Tsinghua University   2 ByteDance Research [ ](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.02359) [ ](https://huggingface.co/Yang130/DeeR-VLA/tree/main) ## Overview This is also the official code repository for the paper [DeeR-VLA](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.02359). DeeR-VLA is a framework for dynamic inference of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) designed specifically for efficient robot execution. Developing MLLMs for real-world robots is challenging due to the typically limited computation and memory capacities available on robotic platforms. DeeR-VLA addresses this challenge by implementing a Dynamic Early-Exit strategy that automatically adjusts the size of the activated MLLM based on the complexity of each situation. This approach leverages a multi-exit architecture, allowing the model to terminate processing once an appropriate size has been activated, thus avoiding redundant computation. DeeR-VLA also incorporates novel algorithms to establish early-termination criteria based on predefined demands such as average computational cost, peak computational consumption, and GPU memory usage. On the CALVIN robot manipulation benchmark, DeeR demonstrates significant reductions in computational costs of LLM by 5.2-6.5x and GPU memory of LLM by 2-6x without compromising performance. ### Performance on CALVIN Benchmark ## Installation 1. Install [CALVIN](https://github.com/mees/calvin) simulator in your python environment following the instructions. Then download dataset `D`, `ABC`, and `ABCD`. 2. Clone DeeR-VLA: 3. Install dependencies in virtual environment: 4. Download the following models from Hugging Face. The OpenFlamingo framework is built on the MPT language model: - OpenFlamingo-3B-vitl-mpt1b-langinstruct
abstract_zh: DeeR-VLA是一个专为高效机器人执行而设计的多模态大语言模型（MLLM）动态推理框架。由于机器人平台通常计算和内存资源有限，开发适用于真实机器人的MLLM颇具挑战。DeeR-VLA通过实施动态早退策略来解决这一问题，该策略根据每个情境的复杂度自动调整激活的MLLM规模。该方法利用多出口架构，使得模型在激活适当规模后即可终止处理，从而避免冗余计算。DeeR-VLA还引入了新颖的算法，基于预定义需求（如平均计算成本、峰值计算消耗和GPU内存使用）建立早退标准。在CALVIN机器人操作基准上，DeeR在不牺牲性能的情况下，实现了LLM计算成本降低5.2-6.5倍、GPU内存降低2-6倍。
tldr_zh: DeeR-VLA通过动态早退策略，在机器人任务中大幅降低计算和内存开销。
cover_zh: 机器人也能“偷懒”｜动态早退让大模型计算量暴降6倍

judge.reason: 官方代码，动态推理MLLM提升机器人执行效率，NeurIPS 24，CALVIN上显著降低计算开销
